spec_match.rationale: The demo now models the WHATWG mimesniff JSON MIME type rule, including lowercase essence parsing, parameter stripping, application/json, text/json, and subtype +json recognition.

interactive.rationale: The select, custom input, and classify button all drive a live before/after matrix and classifier summary rather than a static code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature card and concept lede now describe the same Chrome 138 allow-list versus Chrome 139 spec-aligned +json recognition shown by the page.

edge_cases.rationale: Presets and custom input cover uppercase plus parameters, real +json types, non-JSON suffixes, application/json with parameters, text/plain, and malformed MIME strings.

references.rationale: The page links to chromestatus.com/feature/5470594816278528 and the WHATWG mimesniff JSON MIME type section.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, pairs local button background and text colors, and keeps result table contrast readable.

summary: The old open questions are addressed: the ChromeStatus link is present, the demo avoids unrelated fetch().json() claims, and the interactive matrix now focuses on the spec's JSON MIME type algorithm and its edge cases. Chrome DevTools MCP was not available in this session, so no browser-verification claim is made here.
